attributes.name: base
attributes.description: On <we-route-view>: base path prepended to all route patterns.

attributes.name: transition
attributes.description: On <we-route-view>: enable the View Transitions API for route changes.

attributes.name: keep-alive
attributes.description: On <we-route-view>: cache deactivated route content (ViewEngine hidden modes) instead of destroying it.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: On <we-route-outlet>: the named outlet identifier (empty string for the primary outlet).

attributes.name: route
attributes.description: On a child <template>: URLPattern path to match (e.g. /users/:id).

attributes.name: route:guard
attributes.description: On a child <template>: canActivate guard name, resolved from the @routeGuard context.

attributes.name: route:guard:leave
attributes.description: On a child <template>: canDeactivate guard name, resolved from the @routeGuard context.

attributes.name: route:loader
attributes.description: On a child <template>: loader function name, resolved from the @routeLoader context.

attributes.name: route:error
attributes.description: On a child <template>: marks the template as the error boundary for its route.

attributes.name: route:outlet
attributes.description: On a child <template>: target named outlet (omit for the primary outlet).

attributes.name: lazy
attributes.description: On a child <template>: lazy-load the route content on first navigation.

attributes.name: route:link
attributes.description: On <a> (RouteLinkBehavior): target path to navigate to; intercepts clicks to use the Navigation API and manages the .active class.

attributes.name: route:prefetch
attributes.description: On <a> (RoutePrefetchBehavior): prefetch eagerness ('none' | 'hover' | 'visible' | 'eager'); paired with route:link.

properties.name: scroll
properties.description: Gets or sets the scroll-restoration mode on <we-route-view> ('auto' | 'manual' | 'none').

properties.name: base
properties.description: Gets or sets the base path prepended to all route patterns on <we-route-view>.

properties.name: transition
properties.description: Gets or sets whether the View Transitions API is used for route changes on <we-route-view>.

properties.name: keepAlive
properties.description: Gets or sets whether deactivated route content is cached instead of destroyed on <we-route-view>.

properties.name: routes
properties.description: Gets the read-only list of route definitions parsed from <we-route-view>'s child <template> elements.

properties.name: currentRoute
properties.description: Gets the currently matched and active route on <we-route-view> (null before the first navigation).

properties.name: activeContent
properties.description: Gets the currently stamped route content element on <we-route-outlet> (null when empty).

properties.name: isActive
properties.description: Gets whether this link’s route is currently active (RouteLinkBehavior on the host <a>).

name: Router
summary: Declarative client-side routing built on Navigation API, URLPattern, and ViewEngine.

viewIntegration.description: <route-view> uses ViewEngine directly — it does its own structural DOM stamping in-place (or into named outlets) but delegates hide/show mechanics to ViewEngine.

uses.ViewEngine: Hidden modes for keep-alive cached routes inside <route-view> (or named outlets), toggle events (beforetoggle/toggle), View Transitions API integration
uses.ViewBehavior: Not used — router content doesn't exist until a route matches, no pre-existing elements to attach behavior to
uses.ViewIfDirective: Not used — router has its own URL-based matching, not expression-based conditionals
uses.ViewSwitchDirective: Not used — router manages its own outlet stamping

viewIntegration.rationale: Route content shouldn't exist in DOM until the URL matches. No placeholders, no ghost elements. <route-view> does structural work in-place; ViewEngine provides shared show/hide mechanics.

navigationAPI.pattern: Modern API replacing History API with events, traversal info, and interception
navigationAPI.adoption: Primary navigation mechanism, fallback to History API for Safari <17.2

urlPatternAPI.pattern: URLPattern for route pattern matching with named groups
urlPatternAPI.adoption: Use for all route pattern matching, enables :param and * wildcards

viewTransitionsAPI.adoption: Opt-in via route:transition attribute for smooth navigation

speculationRulesAPI.adoption: Generate speculation rules from route definitions for prefetching

historyAPI.adoption: Fallback only for browsers without Navigation API

hybridArchitecture.description: Router uses a hybrid pattern: components for structural boundaries, behaviors for enhancing existing elements, plain HTML attributes for declarative metadata. Primary rendering is in-place inside <route-view>.

components.RouteViewElement: Custom element (<route-view>). Orchestrator that owns child templates, listens to Navigation API, and stamps matched content in-place as its own children. The router renders where you define the routes.
components.RouteOutletElement: Custom element (<route-outlet>). Named auxiliary views only — for cases where a route renders content to a different area outside the <route-view>.

behaviors.RouteLinkBehavior: CustomAttribute on <a> (route:link). Must preserve native anchor semantics.
behaviors.RoutePrefetchBehavior: CustomAttribute on <a> (route:prefetch). Metadata paired with route:link.

plainAttributes.description: route, route:guard, route:guard:leave, route:loader, route:error, route:outlet on <template> elements are plain HTML attributes read by <route-view> via querySelectorAll. No classes or registry entries needed.
plainAttributes.rationale: Eliminates RouteAttribute, RouteGuardAttribute, RouteLoaderAttribute, RouteErrorAttribute classes. The config component owns template parsing.

hybridArchitecture.rationale: In-place rendering is the simplest model — the router renders where you define the routes. Named outlets exist only for auxiliary views (sidebar, header, footer). Links must stay as <a> elements for accessibility.

naming.rationale: Noun-based prefix consistent with view: and on: conventions. Component tag names use route- prefix. Template attributes are plain HTML read by the view component — no registry needed.

nomenclature.outlet: Named insertion point for routed content (from React Router/Angular)
nomenclature.rationale: Terms from React Router and Angular Router, widely understood

patternSyntax.description: Route patterns follow URLPattern API syntax
patternSyntax.rationale: URLPattern is a web standard, no need for custom syntax

outlets.primary: <route-view> renders matched content in-place — no separate outlet needed for the primary path
outlets.named: Named outlets (<route-outlet name="...">)  for auxiliary views only (sidebars, headers, footers)
outlets.rationale: In-place rendering is the simplest model. Named outlets exist for the special case where a route needs to render content outside the <route-view>.

links.rationale: Declarative links enable pre-rendering and active state styling

route.providedTo: Stamped content inside <route-view> (or named <route-outlet>), set on the element's injector
route.shape: RouteContext { params, data, error, path, query, hash, state }

loaderIntent.description: Declares how route loading states affect the UI. Router resolves this from the injector chain and selects matching Resource Loader traits. Falls back to soft blocking (aria-busy + CSS) when not provided.

contextProviders.rationale: All router data flows through customContexts, consistent with the framework's @ prefix convention. Guards and loaders are named functions resolved from their respective contexts, not magic globals. The loaderIntent context is consumed (not provided) by the router — it reads the app's declared loading UX profile.

guards.description: Plain functions (RouteGuardFn), not class instances. Provided via customContexts:routeGuard, resolved by name from route:guard and route:guard:leave attribute values.
guards.noAbstractClass: The Navigation API's navigate event already provides the guard mechanism — event.intercept() to proceed, event.preventDefault() to cancel, event.signal to abort. An abstract RouteGuard class would duplicate platform-level control. Guard functions are stateless decision points; the injector/context system handles DI via closures.

navigationApiMapping.event.intercept({ handler }): Guard returns true, proceed with navigation pipeline
navigationApiMapping.event.signal: Passed to loaders for automatic abort on navigation supersede

guards.rationale: Functional guards follow Angular v15+ direction (away from class-based CanActivate). Simpler, composable, testable. Navigation API is the single integration point — Router wraps it, not duplicates it.

loaders.description: Named async functions provided via customContexts:routeLoader, resolved by name from route:loader attribute value
loaders.integration: Router resolves customContexts:loaderIntent from the injector chain. The intent's strategy dimension selects which Resource Loader trait controls the loading UX (soft, hard, replacement, optimistic). Per-route overrides via route:loader-strategy, route:loader-context, route:loader-timing attributes.
loaders.rationale: Inspired by React Router loaders, Remix, TanStack Router. Context-based resolution keeps loaders injectable and testable. The Loader Intent bridge means apps declare loading UX once; all consumers (router, forms, data grids) adapt consistently.

errorHandling.description: Error boundary for route loading failures
errorHandling.rationale: Clean error UI without try-catch in user code

lazyLoading.description: Code-split route components, load on navigation

scrollRestoration.rationale: Navigation API provides native scroll restoration

prefetching.rationale: Predictive loading for instant navigation feel

route-change.description: Fired when the active route changes

route-load-start.description: Fired when a route loader begins fetching data

route-load-end.description: Fired when a route loader finishes fetching data

route-error.description: Fired when a route loader fails

events.rationale: Module-prefixed names ('route-change') reduce collision risk. Typed event classes with provider keys enable listenForScoped discrimination for apps embedding multiple routers or combining routers with other blocks that fire change/error events.

sveltekit.notes: File-based but concepts translate to declarative
